entities.name: mInOut

fields.name: organization
fields.label: Organization
fields.help: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

fields.name: documentNo
fields.label: Document No.
fields.help: The document number is usually automatically generated by the system and determined by the document type of the document. If the document is not saved, the preliminary number is displayed in "<>". If the document type of your document has no automatic document sequence defined, the field will be empty when creating a new document. This is for documents which usually have an external number (like vendor invoice).  If you leave the field empty, the system will generate a document number for you. The document sequence used for this fallback number is defined in the "Document Sequence" window with the name "DocumentNo_<TableName>", where TableName is the actual name of the table inside the database (e.g. C_Order).

fields.name: documentType
fields.label: Document Type
fields.help: The Document Type determines document sequence and processing rules

validationRule.code:
C_DocType.DocBaseType IN ('MMR', 'MMS') AND C_DocType.IsSOTrx='@IsSOTrx@' AND AD_ISORGINCLUDED(@AD_Org_ID@,C_DocType.AD_Org_ID, @#AD_Client_ID@) <> '-1'
AND (COALESCE(@isReturnMaterial@, 'N') = C_DocType.IsReturn OR COALESCE(@isDocumentTypeWindow@, 'N') = 'Y')

fields.name: warehouse
fields.label: Warehouse
fields.help: The Warehouse identifies a unique Warehouse where products are stored or Services are provided.

reference.hql:
select w.id as id, w.name as name
from Warehouse w
where @additional_filters@
and w.active=true

fields.name: businessPartner
fields.label: Business Partner
fields.help: A Business Partner is anyone with whom you transact.  This can include a customer, vendor, employee or any combination of these.

fields.name: partnerAddress
fields.label: Partner Address
fields.help: The Partner address indicates the location of a Business Partner

fields.name: movementDate
fields.label: Movement Date
fields.help: The Movement Date indicates the date that a product moved in or out of inventory.  This is the result of a shipment, receipt or inventory movement.

fields.name: accountingDate
fields.label: Accounting Date
fields.help: The date this transaction is recorded on in the general ledger. This date also indicates which accounting period within the fiscal year this transaction will be part of.

fields.name: salesOrder
fields.label: Purchase Order
fields.help: Unique identifier and a reference to a Purchase Order originating from the document sequence defined for this document type.

fields.name: orderReference
fields.label: Order Reference
fields.help: This can be used to input a reference for this specific transaction. For example, a Purchase Order number can be input on a Sales Order for easier reference.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.help: A description is limited to 255 characters.

fields.name: documentStatus
fields.label: Document Status
fields.help: The Document Status indicates the status of a document at this time.  To change the status of a document, use one of the buttons usually located at the bottom of the document window.

fields.name: userContact
fields.label: User/Contact
fields.help: The User identifies a unique user in the system. This could be an internal user or a business partner contact

fields.name: createLinesFrom
fields.label: Create Lines From
fields.help: A process that allows the user to copy lines (products) from an existing document different from the one being copied into. For example, in case of entering a new Goods Shipment, the user can copy lines (products) from an existing Sales Order or Sales Invoice.

fields.name: generateTo
fields.label: Generate Invoice from Receipt
fields.help: Generate Invoice from Receipt will create an invoice based on the selected receipt and match the invoice to that receipt. If the receipt line is related to an existing order line, then the price of the invoice line is taken from that order line. In other case, it uses the retrieved price from the price list selected in the below drop-down list.

fields.name: processGoodsJava
fields.label: Process Shipment Java
fields.help: This process will move products in/out of inventory and mark line items as received/shipped.

fields.name: documentAction
fields.label: Process Shipment
fields.help: This process will move products in/out of inventory and mark line items as received/shipped.

fields.name: posted
fields.label: Posted
fields.help: An accounting status button that indicates if the transaction has already been posted to the general ledger or not. When doing the accounting manually this button can be used to post or unpost the transaction from the general ledger by hand.

fields.name: salesRepresentative
fields.label: Company Agent
fields.help: Purchase agent for the document. Any Sales Rep must be a valid internal user.

reference.filterExpression: EXISTS (SELECT * FROM C_BPartner bp WHERE AD_User.C_BPartner_ID=bp.C_BPartner_ID AND bp.IsSalesRep='Y')

fields.name: charge
fields.label: Charge
fields.help: The Charge indicates a type of Charge (Handling, Shipping, Restocking)

fields.name: chargeAmount
fields.label: Charge Amount
fields.help: The Charge Amount indicates the amount for an additional charge.

fields.name: deliveryTerms
fields.label: Delivery Terms
fields.help: The Delivery Rule indicates when an order should be delivered. For example should the order be delivered when the entire order is complete, when a line is complete or as the products become available.

fields.name: deliveryMethod
fields.label: Delivery Method
fields.help: The Delivery Via indicates how the products should be delivered. For example, will the order be picked up or shipped.

fields.name: freightAmount
fields.label: Freight Amount
fields.help: The Freight Amount indicates the amount charged for Freight in the document currency.

fields.name: freightCostRule
fields.label: Freight Cost Rule
fields.help: The Freight Cost Rule indicates the method used when charging for freight.

fields.name: priority
fields.label: Priority
fields.help: The Priority indicates the importance (high, medium, low) of this document

fields.name: movementType
fields.label: Movement Type
fields.help: The Movement Type indicates the type of movement (in, out, to production, etc)

fields.name: shippingCompany
fields.label: Shipping Company
fields.help: The Shipper is any entity which will provide shipping to or shipping from an Organization.

fields.name: numberOfPackages
fields.label: Number of Packages
fields.help: The number of packages being shipped.

fields.name: activity
fields.label: Activity
fields.help: Activities indicate tasks that are performed and used to utilize Activity based Costing

fields.name: salesCampaign
fields.label: Sales Campaign
fields.help: The Campaign defines a unique marketing program.  Projects can be associated with a pre defined Marketing Campaign.  You can then report based on a specific Campaign.

fields.name: trxOrganization
fields.label: Trx Organization
fields.help: The organization which performs or initiates this transaction (for another organization).  The owning Organization may not be the transaction organization in a service bureau environment, with centralized services, and inter-organization transactions.

fields.name: freightCategory
fields.label: Freight Category
fields.help: Freight Categories are used to calculate the Freight for the Shipper selected

fields.name: calculateFreight
fields.label: Calculate Freight Amount
fields.help: Calculate Freight Amount

fields.name: freightCurrency
fields.label: Freight Currency
fields.help: Indicates the currency of the freight amount when it is calculated.

fields.name: project
fields.label: Project
fields.help: Identifier of a project defined within the Project & Service Management module.

validationRule.code: C_Project.projectstatus in ('OP', 'OR') and (C_Project.c_bpartner_id=@C_BPartner_ID@ or '@IsSOTrx@'='N') or C_Project.c_project_id = @c_project_id@

fields.name: costcenter
fields.label: Cost Center
fields.help: Division that adds to the cost of an organization. Internal structure for cost allocation.

fields.name: asset
fields.label: Asset
fields.help: Any item of economic value owned by the corporation, especially that which could be converted to cash.

fields.name: stDimension
fields.label: 1st Dimension
fields.help: The user defined element displays the optional elements that have been defined for this account combination.

fields.name: ndDimension
fields.label: 2nd Dimension
fields.help: The user defined element displays the optional elements that have been defined for this account combination.

fields.name: eMETBLKCBulkcompletion
fields.label: Bulk Completion
fields.help: You can find the current status of each document in the Document Status field. This action can be performed on multiple records at once.

fields.name: shipmentReceipt
fields.label: Goods Receipt
fields.help: The Receipt ID indicates the unique document for this receipt.

fields.name: pickDate
fields.label: Pick Date
fields.help: Date/Time when picked for Shipment

fields.name: processNow
fields.label: Process Shipment
fields.help: This process will move products in/out of inventory and mark line items as received/shipped.

fields.name: invoice
fields.label: Invoice
fields.help: The Invoice ID uniquely identifies an Invoice Document.

fields.name: client
fields.label: Client
fields.help: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

fields.name: active
fields.label: Active
fields.help:
There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: 
(1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes.
(2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

fields.name: datePrinted
fields.label: Date printed
fields.help: Indicates the Date that a document was printed.

fields.name: salesTransaction
fields.label: Sales Transaction
fields.help: The Sales Transaction checkbox indicates if this item is a Sales Transaction.

fields.name: print
fields.label: Print
fields.help: The Printed checkbox indicates if this document or line will included when printing.

fields.name: trackingNo
fields.label: Tracking No
fields.help: Number to track the shipment

fields.name: logistic
fields.label: Logistic
fields.help: If is logistic it won't be invoiced. It uses for companies that rent locators or incoming materials that we don't want to invoice

fields.name: updateLines
fields.label: Update Attributes from Shipment
fields.help: Update attributes from shipment

fields.name: deliveryLocation
fields.label: Delivery Location
fields.help: Location where the product will be delivered to

fields.name: shipDate
fields.label: Ship Date
fields.help: Actual Date/Time of Shipment (pick up)

fields.name: orderDate
fields.label: Order Date
fields.help: Indicates the Date an item was ordered.

entities.hqlWhereClause: e.movementType IN ('V-', 'V+') and e.logistic = 'N' and e.documentType.return=false

fields.name: lineNo
fields.label: Line No.

derivation.source: SELECT COALESCE(MAX(Line),0)+10 AS DefaultValue FROM M_InOutLine WHERE M_InOut_ID=@M_InOut_ID@

fields.defaultValue: @SQL=SELECT COALESCE(MAX(Line),0)+10 AS DefaultValue FROM M_InOutLine WHERE M_InOut_ID=@M_InOut_ID@
fields.help: Indicates the unique line for a document.  It will also control the display order of the lines within a document.

fields.name: product
fields.label: Product
fields.help: Identifies an item which is either purchased or sold in this organization.

fields.name: attributeSetValue
fields.label: Attribute Set Value
fields.help: The values of the actual Product Attributes

fields.name: operativeQuantity
fields.label: Operative Quantity
fields.help: The number of a certain item involved in the transaction, according to the defined Operative UOM.

fields.name: operativeUOM
fields.label: Alternative UOM
fields.help: The Alternative UOM defines a unique non monetary unit of measure

validationRule.code: td0.c_uom_id IN(select p.c_uom_id from m_product p where p.m_product_id = @M_Product_ID@) or td0.c_uom_id IN(select a.c_uom_id from m_product_aum a where a.m_product_id = @M_Product_ID@ and ((@IsSOTrx@ = 'Y' and a.sales <> 'NA') OR (@IsSOTrx@ <> 'Y' and a.purchase <> 'NA')))

fields.name: movementQuantity
fields.label: Movement Quantity
fields.help: The Movement Quantity indicates the quantity of a product that has been moved.

fields.name: uOM
fields.label: UOM
fields.help: The UOM defines a unique non monetary unit of measure

fields.name: storageBin
fields.label: Storage Bin
fields.help: The Locator ID indicates where in a Warehouse a product is located.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.help: A description is limited to 255 characters.

fields.name: salesOrderLine
fields.label: Purchase Order Line
fields.help: The Purchase Order Line is a unique identifier for a line in an order.

fields.name: orderUOM
fields.label: Order UOM
fields.help: Alternative order UOM for the product.

fields.name: orderQuantity
fields.label: Order Quantity
fields.help: Product quantity in the order uom.

fields.name: organization
fields.label: Organization
fields.help: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

validationRule.code: AD_ISORGINCLUDED(ad_org.ad_org_id, CASE WHEN (@Parent_AD_Org@ IS NULL OR @Parent_AD_Org@ = '') THEN @AD_ORG_ID@ ELSE @Parent_AD_Org@ END, ad_org.ad_client_id)<>-1 AND IsReady='Y'

fields.label: Explode
fields.help: This process will explode the product of the selected line.

fields.name: invoiceQuantity
fields.label: Invoiced Quantity
fields.help: The Invoiced Quantity indicates the quantity of a product that have been invoiced.

fields.name: reinvoice
fields.label: Re-invoice
fields.help: Indicator if invoiced

fields.name: client
fields.label: Client
fields.help: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

fields.name: active
fields.label: Active
fields.help:
There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: 
(1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes.
(2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

fields.name: shipmentReceipt
fields.label: Goods Receipt
fields.help: The Receipt ID indicates the unique document for this receipt.

fields.name: goodsShipmentLine
fields.label: Goods Receipt Line
fields.help: The Goods Receipt Line indicates a unique line in a Receipt document

fields.name: descriptionOnly
fields.label: Description Only
fields.help: If a line is Description Only, e.g. Product Inventory is not corrected. No accounting transactions are created and the amount or totals are not included in the document.  This for including descriptional detail lines, e.g. for an Work Order.

fields.help: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

fields.label: Organization
fields.help: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

fields.name: active
fields.label: Active
fields.help:
There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: 
(1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes.
(2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

fields.name: recordID
fields.label: Record ID
fields.help: The Record ID is the internal unique identifier of a record

fields.name: accountingSchema
fields.label: General Ledger
fields.help: The book containing all financial transactions recorded for the legal entity.

fields.name: currency
fields.label: Currency
fields.help: Indicates the currency to be used when processing this document.

fields.name: period
fields.label: Period
fields.help: The Period indicates an exclusive range of dates for a calendar.

fields.name: accountingDate
fields.label: Accounting Date
fields.help: The date this transaction is recorded on in the general ledger. This date also indicates which accounting period within the fiscal year this transaction will be part of.

fields.name: account
fields.label: Account
fields.help: The (natural) account used

fields.name: sequenceNumber
fields.label: Sequence Number
fields.help: The Sequence indicates the order of records

fields.name: value
fields.label: Value
fields.help: Value of the account used in the entry.

fields.name: accountingEntryDescription
fields.label: Accounting entry Description
fields.help: Description of the accounting entry

fields.name: debit
fields.label: Debit
fields.help: The Account Debit Amount indicates the transaction amount converted to this organization's accounting currency

fields.name: credit
fields.label: Credit
fields.help: The Account Credit Amount indicates the transaction amount converted to this organization's accounting currency

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.help: A description is limited to 255 characters.

fields.help: A Business Partner is anyone with whom you transact.  This can include a customer, vendor, employee or any combination of these.

fields.label: 1st Dimension
fields.help: The user defined element displays the optional elements that have been defined for this account combination.

fields.name: ndDimension

fields.help: The user defined element displays the optional elements that have been defined for this account combination.

fields.name: uOM
fields.label: UOM
fields.help: The UOM defines a unique non monetary unit of measure

fields.name: documentType
fields.label: Document Type
fields.help: The Document Type determines document sequence and processing rules

fields.name: table
fields.label: Table
fields.help: The Table indicates the table in which a field or fields reside.

fields.name: foreignCurrencyDebit
fields.label: Foreign Currency Debit
fields.help: The Source Debit Amount indicates the credit amount for this line in the source currency.

fields.name: salesCampaign
fields.label: Sales Campaign
fields.help: The Campaign defines a unique marketing program.  Projects can be associated with a pre defined Marketing Campaign.  You can then report based on a specific Campaign.

fields.name: locationToAddress
fields.label: Location to Address
fields.help: The Location To indicates the location that a product was moved to.

fields.name: postingType
fields.label: Posting Type
fields.help: The Posting Type indicates the type of amount (Actual, Encumbrance, Budget) this journal updated.

fields.name: documentCategory
fields.label: Document Category
fields.help: The Document Base Type identifies the base or starting point for a document.  Multiple document types may share a single document base type.

fields.name: activity
fields.label: Activity
fields.help: Activities indicate tasks that are performed and used to utilize Activity based Costing

fields.name: withholding
fields.label: Withholding
fields.help: The Withholding indicates the type of withholding to be calculated.

fields.name: lineID
fields.label: Line ID
fields.help: Internal link

fields.name: storageBin
fields.label: Storage Bin
fields.help: The Locator ID indicates where in a Warehouse a product is located.

fields.name: groupID
fields.label: Group ID
fields.help: Accounting entry group ID. Used to group a set of entries

fields.name: type
fields.label: Type
fields.help: Type of entry (Normal, Closing, Opening, Regularization)

fields.name: locationFromAddress
fields.label: Location from Address
fields.help: The Location From indicates the location that a product was moved from.

fields.name: recordID2
fields.label: Record ID 2
fields.help: Payment the fact refers to.

fields.name: trxOrganization
fields.label: Trx Organization
fields.help: The organization which performs or initiates this transaction (for another organization).  The owning Organization may not be the transaction organization in a service bureau environment, with centralized services, and inter-organization transactions.

fields.name: quantity
fields.label: Quantity
fields.help: The Quantity indicates the number of a specific product or item for this document.

fields.name: transactionDate
fields.label: Transaction Date
fields.help: The Transaction Date indicates the date of the transaction.

fields.name: salesRegion
fields.label: Sales Region
fields.help: The Sales Region indicates a specific area of sales coverage.

fields.name: gLCategory
fields.label: G/L Category
fields.help: The General Ledger Category is an optional, user defined method of grouping journal lines.

fields.name: tax
fields.label: Tax
fields.help: The Tax indicates the type of tax for this document line.

fields.name: foreignCurrencyCredit
fields.label: Foreign Currency Credit
fields.help: The Source Credit Amount indicates the credit amount for this line in the source currency.

entities.hqlOrderByClause: accountingSchema.name, accountingDate, abs(debit) desc, credit desc, value

fields.name: lineNo
fields.label: Line No.

derivation.source:
SELECT COALESCE(MAX(LINE),0)+10 AS DefaultValue FROM M_LC_COST
 WHERE (m_landedcost_id is not null and m_landedcost_id =@m_landedcost_id@) 
   or (m_landedcost_id is null and m_inout_id = @m_inout_id@)

fields.help: Indicates the unique line for a document.  It will also control the display order of the lines within a document.

fields.name: organization
fields.label: Organization
fields.help: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

fields.name: landedCostType
fields.label: Landed Cost Type
fields.help: Defines a type of Landed Cost Amount that can be assigned to a Receipt

fields.name: invoiceLine
fields.label: Invoice Line
fields.help: The Invoice Line uniquely identifies a single line of an Invoice.

reference.filterExpression: (e.product.id = @LCC_Product_ID@ or e.account.id = @LCC_Account_ID@) and e.lineNetAmount <> (SELECT coalesce(sum(lcm.amount), 0) from LandedCostMatched lcm where lcm.invoiceLine.id = e.id) and e.invoice.salesTransaction = 'N'

fields.name: goodsShipment
fields.label: Goods Receipt
fields.help: The Receipt ID indicates the unique document for this receipt.

fields.name: currency
fields.label: Currency
fields.help: Indicates the currency to be used when processing this document.

fields.name: landedCostDistributionAlgorithm
fields.label: Landed Cost Distribution Algorithm
fields.help: Identifies the Algorithm to be used to distribute the Landed Cost amounts through all the Receipt Lines.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.help: A description is limited to 255 characters.

fields.name: landedCost
fields.label: Landed Cost
fields.help: Document that relates Landed Costs like Freights or Custom Taxes to Receipts

fields.name: matched
fields.label: Matched
fields.help: Determines that the Landed Cost Cost is completely matched and processed.

fields.name: isMatchingAdjusted
fields.label: Is Matching adjusted
fields.help: When checked the differences in the Matched documents are processed creating a Cost Adjustment

fields.name: active
fields.label: Active
fields.help:
There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: 
(1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes.
(2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

fields.name: cancelMatching
fields.label: Cancel Matching
fields.help: Process that cancels the Matching of the Cost. In case the difference is cost adjusted the generated Cost Adjustment is canceled.

fields.name: client
fields.label: Client
fields.help: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

fields.name: landedCostCost
fields.label: Landed Cost Cost
fields.help: Each Landed Cost amount to be included in the Landed Cost document.
